Title: Complex engineering of alkaloids structural diversity in Solanum

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: previously assigned as xylosyltransferase, but failed to show in planta activity in this study's N. benthamiana reconstruction
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Canonical GAME2 did not enable α-tomatine production in N. benthamiana. 2. Pathway reconstructions with GAME2 accumulated β1-tomatine/β1-dehydrotomatine instead. 3. The study concludes GAME2 is not the active in planta xylosyltransferase for this context.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: transient expression pathway reconstruction; precursor accumulation by LC-MS; comparison with SlGAME2-NEW
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: reported previously as UDP-xylosyltransferase based on in vitro assay; embedded in chromosome 7 SGA cluster

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: showed in vitro β1-tomatine xylosyltransferase activity but no detectable in planta activity for α-tomatine/dehydrotomatine formation in N. benthamiana
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Solyc11g066670 was identified as an in vitro-active candidate xylosyltransferase. 2. It did not support in planta α-tomatine/dehydrotomatine formation in N. benthamiana. 3. The paper suggests it may act on different compounds despite moderate expression in tomato.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: co-expression-guided candidate screening; recombinant enzyme assay; transient expression comparison; LC-MS metabolite profiling
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: moderate expression in tomato relative to GAME2-NEW

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: α-solamargine-related products; uttroside B; Cpd1/Cpd2-associated side products
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: endogenous activities added glucose moieties and generated side products/new-to-nature compounds during heterologous pathway reconstruction
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Endogenous N. benthamiana activities modified intermediates in reconstructed pathways. 2. They likely added glucose at 27-O in uttroside B pathway and on solasodine-related scaffolds, and contributed to Cpd1/Cpd2 side products. 3. These endogenous reactions expand product diversity but can lower pathway purity.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: metabolite profiling and pathway logic inference during transient expression
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: gene identity not resolved; mentioned as unknown endogenous enzyme activity

Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: steroidal glycoalkaloid biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: tomato SGA glycosylation branch; α-tomatine/dehydrotomatine biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Adds a xylose moiety to β1-tomatine to form α-tomatine; supports corresponding xylosylation step leading to dehydrotomatine
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: antimicrobial and other bioactivities associated with Solanum steroidal glycoalkaloids
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: high relative expression across tomato tissues compared with GAME2 and Solyc11g066670; co-expressed with GAME6, GAME11, GAME1, GAME17, and GAME18
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Agrobacterium-mediated transient expression in leaves; VIGS in Solanum pennellii leaves
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: enables in planta production of α-tomatine and dehydrotomatine; silencing reduces α-tomatine and dehydrotomatine and increases β1-tomatine
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Solyc12g009930 was identified as a functional tomato xylosyltransferase and renamed SlGAME2-NEW. 2. In vitro it converted β1-tomatine to α-tomatine and preferred UDP-xylose over UDP-glucose. 3. In planta expression enabled α-tomatine/dehydrotomatine production in N. benthamiana, and VIGS reduced these products while increasing precursor β1-tomatine.
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: key missing step enabling pathway completion in planta
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: co-expression analysis; recombinant enzyme assay in E. coli; transient expression in Nicotiana benthamiana; VIGS in Solanum pennellii; LC-MS/MS metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: tomato RNA-seq transcriptomics from Tomato Genome Consortium (2012), https://doi.org/10.1038/nature11119; co-expression analysis
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: no intrinsic plant fitness penalty reported; heterologous expression in N. benthamiana also yielded side products Cpd1/Cpd2 due to endogenous activities
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: critical enzyme for reconstructing tomato SGA pathways and engineering α-tomatine/dehydrotomatine and downstream derivatives in plant chassis
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: located on chromosome 12 and not in the known chromosome 7 SGA cluster; showed in planta activity unlike previously assigned GAME2 and Solyc11g066670

Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: steroidal glycoalkaloid and steroidal saponin biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: core steroidal scaffold pathway from cholesterol
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers glucuronic acid onto early cholesterol-derived intermediate in the core pathway
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: required as part of core multigene combinations for production of SGAs and saponins from cholesterol in N. benthamiana
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SlGAME15 was included in all successful core reconstruction modules from cholesterol. 2. It functions early in the core pathway supplying glycosylated precursor for downstream scaffold elaboration. 3. Its inclusion was necessary for engineering multiple Solanum alkaloids and saponins.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: transient combinatorial pathway reconstruction and LC-MS metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: function assignment follows pathway scheme and prior literature cited in this paper

Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: steroidal glycoalkaloid and steroidal saponin biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Hydroxylates cholesterol glucuronide at C-22
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: required in core pathway combinations to produce diverse SGAs and saponins in N. benthamiana
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SlGAME6 was part of the reconstructed core pathway from cholesterol. 2. It provides the C-22 hydroxylated scaffold used for both alkaloid and saponin branches. 3. It supported engineering of tomato, eggplant and S. nigrum metabolites.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: transient combinatorial pathway reconstruction and LC-MS metabolite profiling

Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: steroidal glycoalkaloid and steroidal saponin biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Hydroxylates the steroidal scaffold at C-26 and contributes to formation of 25(S) stereochemistry in tomatidenol-derived products
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: directs formation of 25(S) stereoisomeric tomato-type SGAs and saponins; weakly can also yield minor 25(R) products
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SlGAME8 supported tomato-type 22(S),25(S) SGA production. 2. Replacing SlGAME8 with SmGAME8 switched products to 22(R),25(R) stereoisomers. 3. SlGAME8 also appeared to have weak capacity to generate minor 25(R) products.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: determines scaffold stereochemistry for target product branch
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: gene replacement in transient expression modules; LC-MS metabolite profiling

Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: steroidal glycoalkaloid and steroidal saponin biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Hydroxylates the steroidal scaffold at C-26 to generate 25(R) stereochemistry associated with solasodine-derived products
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: 22(R),25(R)-dehydrotomatine; 22(R),25(R)-tomatine; α-solasonine; α-solamargine; dehydrouttroside B; uttroside B
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: switches pathway output to eggplant-type 25(R) stereoisomers and enables biosynthesis of solasodine-derived SGAs and uttroside B-type saponins
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SmGAME8 redirected pathway products from tomato-type to eggplant-type stereochemistry. 2. It enabled formation of 22(R),25(R)-tomatine/dehydrotomatine stereoisomers. 3. It was essential for engineering α-solasonine, α-solamargine and uttroside B-type products.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: determines scaffold stereochemistry for target product branch
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: gene replacement in transient expression modules; LC-MS metabolite profiling

Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: steroidal glycoalkaloid and steroidal saponin biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Hydroxylates steroidal pathway intermediate at C-16
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: multiple SGAs and saponins engineered in this study
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: required as core enzyme in reconstruction modules for steroidal metabolite production
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SlGAME11 was part of all core pathway combinations. 2. It contributed to scaffold formation for both alkaloid and saponin branches. 3. It supported engineering of diverse Solanum steroidal metabolites in N. benthamiana.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: transient combinatorial pathway reconstruction and LC-MS metabolite profiling

Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: steroidal glycoalkaloid biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Oxidizes steroidal intermediate at C-26 in the alkaloid branch
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: required in alkaloid engineering modules but omitted from saponin pathway reconstruction
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SlGAME4 was included in all alkaloid-focused core modules. 2. It was omitted in saponin engineering, consistent with divergence before nitrogen introduction. 3. Its inclusion supported production of multiple SGAs from cholesterol.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: transient combinatorial pathway reconstruction and LC-MS metabolite profiling

Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: steroidal glycoalkaloid biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Introduces nitrogen into the steroidal scaffold during SGA formation
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: required in alkaloid engineering modules; excluded from saponin engineering to avoid nitrogen introduction
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SlGAME12 was essential for nitrogenous SGA reconstruction. 2. Excluding SlGAME12 enabled steroidal saponin branch production. 3. It thus demarcates the alkaloid versus saponin branch logic in pathway engineering.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: combinatorial inclusion/exclusion in transient pathway reconstruction; LC-MS metabolite profiling

Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: steroidal glycoalkaloid and steroidal saponin glycosylation
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: tomatidine/related steroidal aglycone or glycosylated intermediate
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Adds galactose during sequential assembly of the steroidal glycoside chain
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: part of the UGT module enabling tetraose assembly on steroidal scaffold
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SlGAME1 was required in glycosyltransferase modules for tomato SGAs. 2. Together with GAME17, GAME18 and GAME2-NEW it enabled completion of glycoside chains in reconstructed pathways. 3. The paper also leveraged reported promiscuity of tomato UGTs on multiple scaffolds.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: transient combinatorial pathway reconstruction; prior in planta functional context cited; LC-MS metabolite profiling

Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: steroidal glycoalkaloid and steroidal saponin glycosylation
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Adds glucose during sequential assembly of the C-3 glycoside chain
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: α-tomatine; dehydrotomatine; α-solasonine; uttroside B-type products
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: required in glycosylation module and acts on multiple steroidal scaffolds
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SlGAME17 was part of all successful tomato UGT modules. 2. It contributed to glycosylation of both tomatidenol- and solasodine-derived scaffolds. 3. The work supports promiscuity of tomato UGTs on multiple steroidal backbones.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: transient combinatorial pathway reconstruction; LC-MS metabolite profiling

Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: steroidal glycoalkaloid and steroidal saponin glycosylation
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Adds glucose in the late C-3 glycosylation sequence producing β1-tomatine precursor to α-tomatine
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: forms immediate precursor β1-tomatine prior to SlGAME2-NEW action
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SlGAME18 activity led to accumulation of β1-tomatine/β1-dehydrotomatine when GAME2 function was absent. 2. It therefore generates the immediate substrate for SlGAME2-NEW. 3. It is a key component of the tomato UGT module used for pathway engineering.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: transient pathway reconstruction showing precursor accumulation; LC-MS metabolite profiling

Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: steroidal glycoalkaloid and steroidal saponin scaffold modification
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Acts with SlS5αR2 to reduce the C5 double bond, enabling conversion of dehydro forms to saturated forms
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: α-tomatine; α-solamargine; uttroside B; soladulcine A
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: required with SlS5αR2 for production of saturated products such as α-tomatine and uttroside B-type molecules
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Inclusion of SlGAME25 with SlS5αR2 enabled saturated products, while omission yielded dehydro products. 2. It functioned on tomato and eggplant/S. nigrum pathway modules. 3. This pair expanded product diversity across alkaloid and saponin branches.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: combinatorial inclusion/exclusion in transient pathway reconstruction; LC-MS metabolite profiling

Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: steroidal glycoalkaloid and steroidal saponin scaffold modification
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Reduces the C5 double bond in steroidal intermediates, enabling formation of saturated products
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: required with SlGAME25 for formation of saturated SGAs and saponins from corresponding dehydro intermediates
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SlS5αR2 enabled conversion from dehydro to saturated steroidal products. 2. Pairing with SlGAME25 generated α-tomatine, stereoisomeric tomatines, soladulcines and uttroside B-type saponins. 3. Omitting the pair yielded dehydro counterparts.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: combinatorial inclusion/exclusion in transient pathway reconstruction; LC-MS metabolite profiling

Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: ripening-associated tomato SGA modification
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Hydroxylates α-tomatine-derived scaffold at C-23 en route to esculeoside A pathway intermediates
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: acetoxy-hydroxytomatine; acetoxy-hydroxy-dehydrotomatine; esculeoside A pathway intermediates
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: contributed to accumulation of downstream ripening-associated intermediates in N. benthamiana
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SlGAME31 was incorporated into CoreTom2 to extend the α-tomatine pathway toward ripening-associated SGAs. 2. Together with GAME36 and GAME40 it enabled accumulation of acetoxy-hydroxytomatine and acetoxy-hydroxy-dehydrotomatine. 3. Full conversion to esculeoside A was not achieved.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: transient pathway extension and LC-MS metabolite profiling

Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: ripening-associated tomato SGA modification
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Acetylates the C-23 hydroxylated intermediate in the esculeoside A branch
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: helped generate acetylated ripening-associated SGA intermediates in reconstructed pathway
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SlGAME36 was included in the extended CoreTom2 module. 2. It supported accumulation of acetylated hydroxylated tomato SGA intermediates. 3. The pathway stalled before GAME5-dependent final glucosylation.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: transient pathway extension and LC-MS metabolite profiling

Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: ripening-associated tomato SGA modification
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Hydroxylates the C-27 position in the esculeoside A branch
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: its activity was observed via accumulation of GAME40 products in N. benthamiana
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SlGAME40 functioned in the extended tomato pathway in N. benthamiana. 2. Its products acetoxy-hydroxytomatine and acetoxy-hydroxy-dehydrotomatine accumulated. 3. This demonstrated partial reconstitution of the esculeoside A branch.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: transient pathway extension and LC-MS metabolite profiling

Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: ripening-associated tomato SGA modification
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Glucosylates the 27-O position of acetoxy-hydroxytomatine to form esculeoside A
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: no product detected in the multigene N. benthamiana reconstruction, suggesting insufficient substrate availability or poor pathway flux to this terminal step
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SlGAME5 was tested as the terminal 27-O glucosyltransferase of esculeoside A biosynthesis. 2. Despite inclusion in CoreTom2, esculeoside A was not detected. 3. The paper suggests limited substrate availability after expression of 16 genes may have restricted this reaction.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: transient pathway extension and LC-MS metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: terminal step failed in complex multigene reconstruction

Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: steroidal glycoalkaloid scaffold diversification
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Performs desaturation on α-tomatine and dehydrotomatine backbones to form prashantoside A and B
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: enabled production of new-to-nature prashantosides in N. benthamiana
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SlGAME33 converted dehydrotomatine to prashantoside B and α-tomatine to prashantoside A in planta. 2. It expanded scaffold diversity beyond native tomato products. 3. This demonstrated the utility of 2-ODDs for combinatorial SGA diversification.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: transient expression with CoreTom1; substrate feeding controls; LC-MS/MS metabolite profiling

Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: steroidal glycoalkaloid scaffold diversification
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Rearranges the F-ring of α-tomatine and dehydrotomatine to form habrochaitoside A and B
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: enabled production of wild-tomato-type SGAs in N. benthamiana
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ShGAME34 converted α-tomatine/dehydrotomatine into habrochaitoside A/B in planta. 2. It provided a route to wild-tomato structural variants. 3. This confirmed 2-ODDs as major drivers of SGA structural diversification.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: transient expression with CoreTom1; substrate feeding controls; LC-MS/MS metabolite profiling

Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: steroidal glycoalkaloid scaffold diversification
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Acts specifically on unsaturated dehydrotomatine-like scaffold; reported as C-16 hydroxylase/solanidane synthesis-associated dioxygenase
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: produced prashantoside B from dehydrotomatine-producing module but showed no detectable activity on α-tomatine scaffold
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. StDPS generated prashantoside B when co-expressed with dehydrotomatine-producing CoreTom1. 2. It did not produce prashantoside A from α-tomatine-producing conditions. 3. The results suggest substrate preference for unsaturated scaffolds containing the C5,6 double bond.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: transient expression with CoreTom1 ± SlGAME25/SlS5αR2; LC-MS metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: paper interprets strong specificity for unsaturated scaffold

Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: eggplant steroidal glycoalkaloid biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Participates with eggplant UGT module in glycosylation of solasodine-derived scaffold to form α-solasonine
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: part of CoreEgg2 enabling efficient α-solasonine production in N. benthamiana
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. UGT73L15 was included in the eggplant UGT module used to produce α-solasonine. 2. Together with UGT73DU2 and UGT93N3 it enabled efficient heterologous pathway reconstruction from cholesterol. 3. This extended the platform to eggplant-specific SGAs.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: transient combinatorial pathway reconstruction; LC-MS metabolite profiling

Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: eggplant and S. nigrum steroidal glycoalkaloid biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: α-solasonine; α-solamargine; β-soladulcine/soladulcine-related products in combinations
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Participates in glycosylation of eggplant-type steroidal scaffolds in heterologous reconstructions
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: shared component of CoreEgg2 and CoreEgg3 modules enabling several eggplant and S. nigrum SGAs
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. UGT73DU2 was used in both α-solasonine and α-solamargine reconstruction modules. 2. It supported production of eggplant and S. nigrum-type SGAs from cholesterol. 3. It is a versatile glycosyltransferase component of the eggplant pathway.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: transient combinatorial pathway reconstruction; LC-MS metabolite profiling

Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: eggplant steroidal glycoalkaloid biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Participates in sugar chain assembly leading to α-solasonine
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: component of CoreEgg2 for efficient α-solasonine reconstruction
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. UGT93N3 was part of the three-UGT module for α-solasonine production. 2. It contributed to successful reconstruction of the eggplant pathway in N. benthamiana. 3. This supports transferability of eggplant pathway genes into heterologous plant chassis.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: transient combinatorial pathway reconstruction; LC-MS metabolite profiling

Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: eggplant and S. nigrum steroidal glycoalkaloid biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: α-solamargine; soladulcine A/malonyl-soladulcine A in combinations
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Participates in glycosylation module leading to α-solamargine and related reduced products
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: part of CoreEgg3 enabling α-solamargine and, with SlGAME25/SlS5αR2, S. nigrum-type reduced products
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. UGT93M2 partnered with UGT73DU2 and SmMAT in CoreEgg3. 2. This module produced α-solamargine and malonyl-solamargine. 3. With SlGAME25/SlS5αR2 it also supported formation of soladulcine A derivatives.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: transient combinatorial pathway reconstruction; LC-MS metabolite profiling

Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: eggplant and S. nigrum steroidal glycoalkaloid biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers a malonyl group to α-solamargine-derived scaffold and related products
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: enabled production of malonylated eggplant and S. nigrum-type SGAs in N. benthamiana
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SmMAT was used with UGT73DU2 and UGT93M2 to produce malonyl-solamargine. 2. In combination with SlGAME25/SlS5αR2 it also supported malonyl-soladulcine A production. 3. It demonstrates terminal acyl decoration can be transplanted into heterologous pathway reconstructions.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: transient combinatorial pathway reconstruction; LC-MS metabolite profiling
